notes: Subsection (a) exempts from Statutory PAYGO scorecards under section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010

provisions.description: Amends the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 (15 U.S.C. 8501 et seq.) by adding a new Title VI—Improving Federal Precipitation Information, establishing requirements for a National Academies study on precipitation estimation, updating probable maximum precipitation estimates, and developing a National Guidance Document.
provisions.raw_text: The Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 (15 U.S.C. 8501 et seq.) is amended by adding at the end the following:
notes: Division D is the PRECIP Act (Providing Research and Estimates of Changes In Precipitation Act)
Adds new Title VI (Sections 601-603) to the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017

cross_references.target: Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 (15 U.S.C. 8501 et seq.)
cross_references.description: Adds new Title VI—Improving Federal Precipitation Information

provisions.description: Requires the Administrator of NOAA, within 90 days of enactment, to seek an agreement with the National Academies to conduct a study on the state of practice and research needs for precipitation estimation, including probable maximum precipitation estimation, and submit a report within 24 months to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology and the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the PRECIP Act, the Administrator, in consultation with other Federal agencies as appropriate, sh
notes: Creates new SEC. 601 of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017
Study must cover 10 areas including current practices, best practices, research needs, observation requirements, and a recommended Federal R&D program plan

cross_references.target: Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017
cross_references.description: New Section 601—Study on precipitation estimation

provisions.raw_text: There is authorized $1,500,000 to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to carry out this study.
notes: This is an authorization of appropriations, NOT an appropriation. It authorizes future appropriations up to this amount.
Found in new SEC. 601(c) of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017

provisions.description: Requires the Administrator of NOAA, within 90 days after publication of the National Academies report, to consult with relevant partners and develop a plan to: (1) update probable maximum precipitation estimates for the United States not later than 6 years after report completion and at least every 10 years thereafter, considering non-stationarity; (2) coordinate research on extreme precipitation estimation; (3) make publicly available all NOAA PMP studies; and (4) preserve and curate all PMP estimate data and products.
deadlines: 90 days after National Academies report publication
6 years after report completion for first PMP update
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 90 days after the date on which the National Academies makes public the report under section 601, the Administrator, in consideration o
notes: Creates new SEC. 602(a) of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017
Updates must consider non-stationarity in precipitation estimates

cross_references.target: Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017
cross_references.description: New Section 602—Improving probable maximum precipitation estimates

provisions.description: Requires the Administrator of NOAA to develop a National Guidance Document for probable maximum precipitation estimates, in collaboration with Federal agencies, State, territorial, Tribal, and local governments, academia, and other partners. The document must provide best practices, consider National Academies recommendations, facilitate regulatory review, and provide confidence in regional and site-specific estimates. Must be published within 2 years of the National Academies report and updated at least every 10 years.
deadlines: 2 years after National Academies report publication
provisions.raw_text: The Administrator, in collaboration with Federal agencies, State, territorial, Tribal and local governments, academia, and other partners the Administra
notes: Creates new SEC. 602(b)-(d) of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017
National Guidance Document to be used by Federal and State regulatory agencies, private meteorological consultants, and other PMP study performers

cross_references.target: Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017
cross_references.description: New Section 602(b)—National Guidance Document for PMP Estimates

provisions.description: Conforming amendment to update the table of contents of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 to include the new Title VI—Improving Federal Precipitation Information and its three sections.
provisions.raw_text: Section 1(b) of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 (15 U.S.C. 8501 note) is amended in the table of contents by adding at the
notes: Conforming amendment in SEC. 2(b) of Division D
Adds entries for SEC. 601, 602, and 603 to the table of contents

cross_references.target: Section 1(b) of the Weather Research and Forecasting Innovation Act of 2017 (15 U.S.C. 8501 note)
cross_references.description: Updates table of contents

flagged_issues: Division A contains only two substantive provisions: a CR date extension and a cybersecurity waiver. All spending authority flows through the original CR (P.L. 117-180, Division A) at prior-year rates.
SEC. 4 (payment of $174,000 to beneficiary of Rep. McEachin) is outside any division and was not extracted per the Division A scope instruction.
All Division B provisions are non-spending policy riders: four short-term authorization extensions (SEC. 101-104) and one budget process exemption (SEC. 201)
SEC. 4 (payment to beneficiary of Rep. McEachin, $174,000) is in the bill preamble before any division and is NOT part of Division B
SEC. 104 reduces Medicare Improvement Fund by $30,000,000 (from $7,308,000,000 to $7,278,000,000) as a budget offset
SEC. 201 and SEC. 202 appropriate indeterminate amounts (pro rata portion and such sums as may be necessary, respectively)
All extensions in Division C move deadlines by approximately 7 days, from December 16/17, 2022 to December 23/24, 2022, consistent with a short-term CR extension
SEC. 2 contains an authorization of appropriations ($1,500,000) for NOAA, not an actual appropriation
Division D (PRECIP Act) is entirely a policy/authorization division with no direct appropriations
SEC. 1 of Division D is the short title and is not extracted per instructions
